page: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v5 is an EDSv5-series virtual machine configured with 8 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading and dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

The instance includes 1229 GB of local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable. In memory benchmarks, the server demonstrates a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 417.04 GB/sec, which drops to 59.66 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, and a write bandwidth of 43.37 GB/sec. This storage capacity and memory throughput support memory-intensive workloads that require fast local scratch space.

Benchmark results place the server in the average performance tier for general compute tasks, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1681.0 and a multi-core score of 6221.0. Database operations are supported by a PassMark database score of 4096.32 and Redis SET throughput reaching 7.97 million operations per second. However, compute-heavy AI workloads show clear limitations; while prompt processing for a small 135M LLM model achieves 679.97 tokens/sec, performance drops to a weak 17.48 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model, and large 70B models are unsupported due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32-8ds_v5 offers high memory density relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for workloads where memory capacity is the primary constraint rather than raw compute power. This configuration is suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data buffering, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM and local temporary storage without the overhead of high vCPU licensing costs.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, featuring 8 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, it provides dedicated CPU allocation and supports AVX-512 instructions. The server includes 1229 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a PassMark database score of 4096.32 and Redis SET throughput of 7.97 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weak performance on medium-sized LLM inference tasks due to the absence of dedicated GPU hardware. This instance is designed for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, and caching layers that require a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v5 is an EDSv5-series virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8ds_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 8 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, and substantial local storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 8 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM
1229 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core
Average performance in database operations and Redis caching
Weak performance on medium-sized LLM inference workloads
Best suited for in-memory databases and memory-constrained applications
